findings: commands/health.md step 1 rewritten: the fleet sweep is now a single 'node ~/.keel/bin/keel-state.cjs status --all' call returning the JSON array of {story_id, scope, current_phase, halted} ([] on empty fleet); halted and {story_id, error} corrupt-manifest entries are flagged from the listing, and per-story 'status <story>' (deep view: sequencing gaps, idle > 48h) plus 'verify <story>' run for flagged stories only. Frontmatter, engine-path note, steps 2-5, and the report format were left untouched.

Conventions writeback: .keel/memory/conventions.md did not exist — created with a dated header and one dated one-liner: 'Engine read-only listings (status --all) are lock-free and skip-and-mark corrupt entries — never abort a sweep for one bad story (ADR-001).'
Memory bounds verified via 'node ~/.keel/bin/keel-state.cjs memory-check': conventions.md 6 lines (cap 150), lessons.md 1 entry (cap 30) — PASS, within bounds.

No README or API-doc changes needed: the CLI contract (stdout/exit codes) is documented in docs/design/KEEL-102-design.md and the sole consumer is commands/health.md; security phase carried 0 HIGH/MEDIUM findings, nothing security-sensitive to redact from docs.
decisions: No CHANGELOG edit this phase: release versioning is the orchestrator's job, so the changelog entry is deferred to the release phase rather than written here.

Kept idle > 48h and sequencing-gap checks in the per-story deep view for flagged stories only, because the status --all projection is deliberately capped at 4 keys (BR-2) and re-running per-story status fleet-wide would defeat the one-call sweep.
Created conventions.md fresh (only lessons.md existed) with the 150-line cap noted in its header, per the technical-writer memory-maintainer mandate.
